methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

data_flows.trust_changes: Borrower input becomes protocol-managed loan state; collateral custody transfers to LendingTerm
exposure_risks: Predictable loanId via keccak256(borrower, term, timestamp) allows front-running
manipulation_risks: Loan ID collision prevents same borrower from borrowing twice in same second on same term
Gauge weight flash manipulation could inflate debt ceiling before borrow

transformations: For partial: percentRepaid = debtToRepay * 1e18 / loanDebt, issuanceDecrease = borrowAmount * percentRepaid / 1e18
data_flows.trust_changes: External CREDIT from repayer flows into protocol; principal burned reduces supply
manipulation_risks: Rounding in partial repay: principalRepaid rounds down, so interestRepaid rounds up slightly, favoring protocol
Strict > in minBorrow check prevents reducing to exactly minBorrow

transformations: Phase 1: collateralReceived = collateralAmount * elapsed / midPoint; creditAsked = callDebt
Phase 2: collateralReceived = collateralAmount; creditAsked = callDebt - callDebt * elapsed / PHASE_2_DURATION
pnl = creditFromBidder >= principal ? int(interest) : int(creditFromBidder) - int(principal)
data_flows.trust_changes: AuctionHouse is trusted caller for LendingTerm.onBid; bidder must approve CREDIT on LendingTerm
validation_points: startAuction: msg.sender has GAUGE_PNL_NOTIFIER role, loan.callTime == block.timestamp
onBid: msg.sender == auctionHouse, loan called but not closed
exposure_risks: MEV extraction: bidder can time bid for maximum collateral / minimum credit
manipulation_risks: At auction end (creditAsked=0), bid() reverts requiring forgive() instead, marking total loss

data_flows.trust_changes: Only GAUGE_PNL_NOTIFIER role can call; CREDIT must be pre-transferred
manipulation_risks: If gaugeWeight is 0 when profit is notified, amountForGuild stays on ProfitManager unclaimed
Profit index precision: amountForGuild * 1e18 / gaugeWeight truncates, dust accumulates

path: Over DISTRIBUTION_PERIOD: share price interpolates upward
On user interaction: _shares2balance computes higher balance, _mint delta to user
transformations: Share price interpolates linearly over 30 days
data_flows.trust_changes: Burned tokens become unminted rebase rewards
validation_points: rebasingSupply != 0 (else tokens burnt with no benefit)
manipulation_risks: Multiple distributions restart the 30-day interpolation, delaying ALL pending rewards
Low rebasing supply enables share price manipulation (similar to vault inflation attack)
First rebaser with small balance can have share truncation issues

path: getRewards: ProfitManager.claimRewards(this) -> compute deltaIndex -> transfer creditReward -> mint guildReward
data_flows.trust_changes: User's CREDIT becomes first-loss capital; GUILD minted for voting power
manipulation_risks: CRITICAL: slashed flag checked against uninitialized userStake memory, always true when lastGaugeLoss > 0
rewardRatio changes retroactively affect all pending unclaimed rewards

data_flows.source: LendingTerm (bad debt from auction or forgive)
path: If loss < surplusBuffer: deplete buffer, burn CREDIT
Else: empty buffer, burn buffer, update creditMultiplier down
data_flows.trust_changes: Loss socializes to all CREDIT holders via creditMultiplier reduction
manipulation_risks: If loss >= creditTotalSupply, subtraction underflows and reverts - loss cannot be processed
creditMultiplier can reach 0 if loss equals totalSupply, bricking all division-by-multiplier calculations
Very small redeemAmountOut can round to 0, user burns CREDIT for nothing
creditMultiplier approaching 0 inflates CREDIT minting from PSM

core_rules: Loans must be sufficiently collateralized: borrowAmount <= collateralAmount * maxDebtPerCollateralToken / creditMultiplier
Issuance per term bounded by hardCap, debt ceiling (gauge allocation * tolerance), and rate limiter
Interest is non-compounding, linear over time, expressed as APR
Losses are absorbed by: 1) term surplus buffer, 2) global surplus buffer, 3) creditMultiplier markdown
GUILD voters for loss-generating terms are slashed (burned)
Rebasing CREDIT holders receive share of interest via ERC20RebaseDistributor
PSM maintains CREDIT peg at creditMultiplier * pegToken value
economic_incentives: Borrowers pay interest split between surplus buffer, GUILD voters, other recipient, and rebasing CREDIT holders
GUILD voters stake tokens to increase debt ceilings and earn proportional interest share
GUILD voters risk slashing if their voted term generates bad debt
SurplusGuildMinter allows CREDIT staking as first-loss capital to earn GUILD voting power at reduced cost
Surplus buffer acts as first-loss capital protecting CREDIT holders from bad debt
governance_mechanisms: Term onboarding via GuildGovernor proposal + timelock with veto capability
Term offboarding via GUILD holder polling (LendingTermOffboarding) with quorum
GuildVetoGovernor allows token holders to cancel queued timelock actions
Governor role controls critical parameters (hardCap, auctionHouse, mintRatio, etc.)
Guardian can pause contracts in emergency

edge_cases.scenario: SurplusGuildMinter.getRewards checks slashed flag against uninitialized userStake memory
edge_cases.expected_behavior: slashed should be true only if user staked before the last gauge loss and hasn't applied it
edge_cases.actual_behavior: slashed is always true when lastGaugeLoss > 0 because userStake.lastGaugeLoss is 0 (default) when checked, before storage is loaded
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Any term with a historical gauge loss causes ALL SurplusGuildMinter stakers on that term to be incorrectly flagged as slashed. In getRewards, their stake is zeroed. In unstake, the function returns early without withdrawing from surplus buffer. User CREDIT in surplus buffer is permanently locked. Anyone can call getRewards(user, term) to trigger this for any user.

edge_cases.scenario: ERC20Gauges._decrementWeightUntilFree has infinite loop when gauge has zero weight
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Loop should always terminate, incrementing i for each gauge
edge_cases.actual_behavior: The unchecked { ++i; } is inside if(userGaugeWeight != 0) block. If a gauge has 0 weight, i never increments, causing infinite loop (gas exhaustion)
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A user calling incrementGauge(gauge, 0) adds a 0-weight gauge to their set. When _decrementWeightUntilFree is triggered (on transfer/burn when weight needs freeing) and the 0-weight gauge is iterated before enough weight is freed, transaction reverts with out of gas. Self-inflicted DoS but could affect SurplusGuildMinter if mintRatio=0.

edge_cases.scenario: LendingTerm.forgive() called on a loan in active auction
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Either forgive or auction should cleanly close the loan, not both
edge_cases.actual_behavior: forgive sets loan.closeTime and decrements issuance. Later, AuctionHouse.bid()/forgive() calls onBid which reverts because closeTime != 0. Auction stuck permanently; nAuctionsInProgress never decremented, blocking setAuctionHouse.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Governor-only action, but incorrect usage permanently blocks auction house migration. Requires emergencyAction on AuctionHouse to fix nAuctionsInProgress counter.

edge_cases.scenario: creditMultiplier reaches zero or near-zero from catastrophic bad debt
edge_cases.expected_behavior: System should gracefully handle extreme losses
edge_cases.actual_behavior: If loss >= creditTotalSupply after buffer depletion, the subtraction (creditTotalSupply - loss) underflows and reverts. If loss equals totalSupply exactly, creditMultiplier becomes 0, causing division by zero in getLoanDebt, getMintAmountOut, etc.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Cannot be directly triggered by an attacker but extreme market conditions or cascading liquidations could push toward this state. System becomes permanently bricked if creditMultiplier = 0.

edge_cases.scenario: LendingTerm._partialRepay uses strict > for minBorrow check
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Borrower should be able to partially repay down to exactly minBorrow
edge_cases.actual_behavior: require(borrowAmount - issuanceDecrease > minBorrow()) uses strict greater-than, preventing reduction to exactly minBorrow
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Not exploitable, but creates a small unusable range near minBorrow boundary. Borrower might need to fully repay instead of partially repaying.

edge_cases.scenario: Loan ID is deterministic and non-reusable after loan closure
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Borrowers should be able to take new loans after repayment
edge_cases.actual_behavior: loanId = keccak256(borrower, term, timestamp). After loan closure, borrowTime remains non-zero, so same loanId can never be used again. Same borrower cannot borrow in the same second on the same term, even after closing the previous loan.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Limits borrower to one loan per second per term. Minor UX issue but could affect automated strategies.

edge_cases.scenario: Profit distribution to gauge with 0 weight
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Guild's share of profit should be distributed or handled gracefully
edge_cases.actual_behavior: If gaugeWeight is 0 when notifyPnL is called with profit, amountForGuild CREDIT tokens remain stuck on ProfitManager with no mechanism to claim them
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Tokens become permanently stuck. Could happen during offboarding if the gauge is deprecated before auction resolution.

edge_cases.scenario: GuildVetoGovernor veto can only be attempted once per timelockId
edge_cases.expected_behavior: If a veto fails to reach quorum, another attempt should be possible
edge_cases.actual_behavior: createVeto generates a deterministic proposalId from timelockId. After a failed veto, the proposal state is Defeated and cannot be recreated with the same parameters.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A malicious actor could create a veto proposal early (when few votes are available) knowing it will fail, preventing future legitimate veto attempts on the same timelock action.

edge_cases.scenario: ERC20RebaseDistributor distribute() resets interpolation period for all pending rewards
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Previously distributed rewards should continue interpolating on their original schedule
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Each new distribute() call resets both __rebasingSharePrice and __unmintedRebaseRewards interpolation to new 30-day period from current timestamp, delaying ALL pending unminted rewards
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Frequent small distributions can perpetually delay reward materialization. An attacker could call distribute(1) repeatedly to delay other users' rewards.

edge_cases.scenario: AuctionHouse.bid requires creditAsked != 0 but at auction end creditAsked becomes 0 discontinuously
edge_cases.actual_behavior: At exactly startTime + auctionDuration, creditAsked jumps to 0 from a small positive value. bid() reverts; only forgive() works, marking total loss even though the collateral has full value
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If all bidders are blocked (e.g., MEV censorship) until the exact second auctionDuration elapses, the loan becomes a total loss unnecessarily.

edge_cases.scenario: LendingTermOffboarding pauses PSM redemptions during offboarding
edge_cases.expected_behavior: PSM redemptions should remain available for credit holders
edge_cases.actual_behavior: First offboarding pauses redemptions. If multiple terms are offboarded, redemptions stay paused until ALL are cleaned up. Long-duration offboardings lock PSM redemptions for extended periods.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: An attacker with enough GUILD to reach offboarding quorum can force PSM redemptions to pause by continuously proposing offboardings. Combined with existing loans that can't be quickly called, this could lock redemptions for months.

assumption_violations: SurplusGuildMinter assumes userStake is loaded before slashed check - VIOLATED
ERC20Gauges assumes all gauges in user set have non-zero weight - VIOLATED when incrementGauge called with weight=0
LendingTerm.forgive assumes loan is not in active auction or handles it - NOT HANDLED for auction state cleanup
ProfitManager assumes creditTotalSupply > loss when calculating new creditMultiplier - can be VIOLATED in extreme scenarios
System assumes creditMultiplier > 0 for all arithmetic - can be VIOLATED if loss equals totalSupply exactly
GuildVetoGovernor assumes veto proposals can be retried - VIOLATED by deterministic proposalId
game_theory_risks: Gauge weight manipulation: user can flash-loan GUILD, vote for their term to inflate debt ceiling, borrow max, then unvote. Mitigated by _incrementGaugeWeight requiring no pending loss and claiming rewards.
SurplusGuildMinter slashing bug creates risk-free griefing: anyone can call getRewards on any user's position on a term with historical loss to zero their stake
Offboarding polling: GUILD holder can snapshot their votes, create poll, vote, then transfer tokens. Snapshot-based so this is by design.
Auction MEV: bidders can time bids to extract maximum value; first phase offers increasing collateral for full debt, second phase decreases debt for all collateral
Borrower can front-run a call by repaying in the same block to avoid auction (prevented by borrowTime < block.timestamp check)
integration_risks: Fee-on-transfer collateral tokens would cause LendingTerm to receive less than collateralAmount, breaking collateral accounting
Rebasing collateral tokens would silently change collateral balances, making collateralAmount in loan struct inaccurate
Non-standard ERC20 tokens (no return value) handled by SafeERC20 but could still have unexpected behavior
External oracle dependency: none (Dutch auction for price discovery), but creditMultiplier acts as internal oracle
CreditToken combines ERC20MultiVotes + ERC20RebaseDistributor creating complex interaction surface for share/vote/balance tracking
calculation_errors: SurplusGuildMinter.getRewards: slashed = (lastGaugeLoss > uint256(userStake.lastGaugeLoss)) checked before userStake is loaded from storage; userStake.lastGaugeLoss is always 0
LendingTerm.getLoanDebt interest calculation order: (borrowAmount * interestRate * time) / YEAR / 1e18 - potential intermediate overflow for very large values though unlikely with uint256
ProfitManager.setProfitSharingConfig truncation: uint32(split / 1e9) loses precision for values not divisible by 1e9; creditSplit absorbs rounding remainder
ProfitManager.claimGaugeRewards: creditEarned = (userGaugeWeight * deltaIndex) / 1e18 truncates; accumulated dust across all users can exceed available balance

token_semantics.notes: Combines ERC20RebaseDistributor (share-based rebasing with 30-day interpolated distribution) and ERC20MultiVotes. balanceOf is virtual for rebasing users. totalSupply includes unminted rebase rewards. Use SafeERC20 when interacting externally. Internal mint/burn/transfer override chain: CreditToken -> ERC20RebaseDistributor -> ERC20MultiVotes (_decrementVotesUntilFree).

Non-transferable by default until governance calls enableTransfer(). ERC20Gauges + ERC20MultiVotes combined. _decrementGaugeWeight checks for pending gauge losses and debt ceiling usage. _incrementGaugeWeight checks for pending losses and claims rewards. _burn calls both _decrementWeightUntilFree and _decrementVotesUntilFree.

Arbitrary ERC20 set per LendingTerm. SafeERC20 used for all transfers. Fee-on-transfer or rebasing tokens would break collateral accounting. Decimals vary; maxDebtPerCollateralToken must account for decimal differences.
Stable coin used for PSM. decimalCorrection = 10^(18 - pegToken.decimals). pegTokenBalance tracked separately to exclude donations. SafeERC20 used.

invariants.name: supply_conservation_credit
invariants.notes: CreditToken.totalSupply() = ERC20.totalSupply() + unmintedRebaseRewards(). The sum of all balanceOf should equal totalSupply (with per-account 1 wei rounding).

invariants.name: rebasing_supply_conservation
invariants.notes: totalSupply == nonRebasingSupply + rebasingSupply. nonRebasingSupply uses max(0, totalSupply - rebasingSupply) to handle rounding.

invariants.name: gauge_weight_conservation
invariants.notes: totalWeight equals sum of weights of all live (non-deprecated) gauges. Deprecated gauges retain weight in storage but excluded from totalWeight.

invariants.name: issuance_tracking
invariants.notes: issuance tracks total borrowAmount of open loans. Partial repays reduce both issuance and loan.borrowAmount. Full repay/onBid/forgive decrements by full remaining borrowAmount.

invariants.name: credit_multiplier_monotonic_decrease
invariants.notes: creditMultiplier can only decrease (through bad debt) or stay constant. Never increases. Starts at 1e18.

invariants.name: surplus_buffer_solvency
invariants.notes: CreditToken balance of ProfitManager should be >= surplusBuffer + sum(termSurplusBuffer) + unclaimed guild rewards

invariants.name: sgm_stake_surplus_buffer_parity
invariants.notes: Sum of all users' credit stakes for a term should equal the termSurplusBuffer for that term (minus any losses). BROKEN by slashing bug.
